title: Exercise 7-4 (Algo) Information Technology Internal Service Fund [LO 7-2]

body.scenario: Washington City created an Information Technology department four years ago to centralize information technology (IT) functions for the city. The goal of the department was to reduce costs, avoid duplication of efforts, and provide up-to-date technology to all of the city’s operations. The fund was designed to be self-supporting; that is, all costs are to be recovered through user fees, but any excess of fees over expenses should be less than 3 percent. The postclosing trial balance for the IT department as of last year, is shown below.
required: Gross employee wages were $54,800, including the employer’s share of social security taxes amounting to $3,910. Federal income and social security taxes withheld from that amount totaled $17,870.
Office expenses in the amount of $3,540 were paid in cash.
Supplies purchased on account during the year were $8,010.
A bill totaling $13,840 was received for utilities provided by Washington City’s utility fund.
Cash paid to the federal government for payroll taxes was $22,100.
Cash paid to the Utility Fund was $13,800.
Materials and supplies used during the year were $7,850.
Charges to departments during the fiscal year were as follows: General Fund $54,800 Special Revenue Fund 19,800
Unpaid balances at year-end were: General Fund $3,500 Special Revenue Fund 1,900
The depreciation for the year was $6,400.
Revenue and expense accounts for the year were closed.
